svg.text: 02 - Product Flow: Planning and Live Companion
What this covers: end-user product behavior, phase transitions, and operational rules Buddi follows.
North Star Product Behavior
Users do not "configure a travel app"; they collaborate with Buddi in conversation to build and continuously improve a trip.
Phase A - Pre-Trip Planning Flow (10 steps)
01) Quick profile (adventure, fitness, pace, interests, budget, group, max driving).
02) Destination + dates + entry/exit + flight context + seasonal intelligence framing.
03) Must-dos and must-not-dos become hard constraints and filters.
04) Interest exploration via concrete curated options, not blank questions.
05) Attraction curation with nationwide provider comparison and diversification logic.
06) Route skeleton: geographic clusters, direction choice, ferry bottlenecks, detour trade-offs.
07) Transport layer: multi-modal recommendations by segment (car/campervan/ferry/flight/bus).
08) Pace and duration calibration with explicit trade-off options if days are constrained.
09) Itinerary assembly as interactive dashboard (not chat dump) with editable day cards.
10) Review/refine loop: reorder, add/remove, lock day, re-validate, then confirm.
Planning Design Rules
- Conversation-first, structured choices, optional free text, and "you decide" autonomy path.
- User constraints are sacred: must-do/must-not-do and locked days always respected.
- 80-90% of initial plan can be AI-generated; user acts as director/editor.
- Route quality targets: low backtracking, no impossible drives, balanced activity intensity.
- Itinerary intelligence includes flex days, weather-sensitive distribution, and drive-time realism.
- Dashboard cards include booking status, tightness score, conditions, transport, accommodation, maps.
Output Contract at Confirmation
Trip status moves from planning to confirmed; monitoring warm-up starts; pre-departure guidance begins.
This handoff state is what activates operational companion workflows downstream.
Phase B - Live Companion Operational Rhythm
Activation timeline:
T-3 days: forecast monitoring starts.
T-1 day: pre-departure briefing and first-day prep.
T-0 onward: full companion mode active.
Daily cadence:
Evening preview (configurable): tomorrow summary, packing prep, departure hint.
Morning briefing (core touchpoint): scores, timing, packing, hidden gems, swap suggestion if needed.
During-day live updates: meaningful changes only; no notification spam.
Evening wrap: recap + next-day adjustment context.
Condition Evaluation Semantics
EXCELLENT / GOOD / FAIR / POOR / UNSAFE with activity-specific factor weighting.
Different activity classes look at different variables (hiking wind chill vs kayaking sea state).
Forecast confidence degrades with horizon, changing how forcefully Buddi recommends changes.
Swap Suggestion Gatekeeping
Suggest only when: meaningful gain + feasible logistics + clear narrative + user pattern-respecting behavior.
Never suggest on trivial deltas, locked-day conflicts, or where no better window exists.
Decision thresholds (example): strong recommendation when improvement >70 and disruption <40.
Responses: accept -> apply and regenerate affected outputs; decline -> record preference and continue.
Lookahead spans 3-5 days to capture weather windows, not just day-of-local optimum.
Safety layer is non-negotiable: severe weather/closure/emergency info bypasses convenience logic.
Feature Matrix by Lifecycle (Operational Ownership View)
Planning-heavy features: onboarding profile, itinerary building, transport design, review/refine dashboard.
Companion-heavy features: daily briefings, condition monitoring, adaptive swaps, activity guidance, alerts.
Cross-cutting features: chat with Buddi, map integration, account persistence, notifications, storytelling.
Implementation Priorities (Product Reality)
MVP-defining: planning conversation, itinerary generation, interactive dashboard, account/auth, map baseline.
V1.1-defining: daily briefings, condition engine, adaptation logic, push notifications, deep guidance.
V2 candidates: richer marketplace, advanced audio companion, broader social/post-trip loops.
Critical Product Risks if Implemented Poorly
- Over-chatty, low-value swap recommendations destroy trust.
- Generic guidance (non-specific tips) breaks the "expert companion" promise.
- Weak route realism (excessive driving/backtracking) makes plans feel fake.
- UI that behaves like static text instead of editable control plane limits adoption.
- Ignoring confidence horizons causes brittle over-optimization and wrong certainty tone.
Success Signals
Users should feel: "Buddi saved us from bad timing and made every day easier, richer, and less stressful."
Operational KPIs: acceptance of high-value swaps, briefing engagement, edit-completion velocity, satisfaction after trip.
Next recommended read: 03 - AI Agent and Decision Engine.
